name: Topig (Lenten Chick Pea Kofta)
recipeInstructions:
Serves: 8-12 Cooking time: 35 minutes
1. Soak chick peas in cold water for 24 hours, keeping them cool if the weather is warm.
2. Remove skins by taking a handful at a time and rubbing with palms of hands so that the peas rub against one another. Return to bowl and repeat until floating skins are removed.
3. Drain well.
4. Process skinned chick peas twice through a food grinder with a fine screen, or in two batches using a food processor.
5. Peel boiled potatoes and mash finely with a fork. Combine with ground chick peas, add salt and white pepper, and blend thoroughly.
6. Steam sliced onions over medium heat until moisture evaporates, then cool and blend.
7. Add allspice, cumin, pine nuts, currants to the blended onion mixture. Mix in tahina, salt, and pepper to taste.
8. Place 4 pieces of unbleached calico (50 cm square) in boiling water for a few seconds, then cool slightly and wring out well.
9. Spread half of chick pea paste evenly on one cloth square to form a 20 cm square.
10. Place a quarter of the filling in the center, then bring each corner over the filling, lifting up corners of the cloth to enclose it.
11. Smooth joins and seal well by making two ties at diagonally opposite corners.
12. Repeat with remaining paste and filling.
13. Boil water in a large pot, add salt, and gently lower prepared topigs into boiling water. Return to boil and cook for 12-15 minutes until they float and feel firm.
14. Lift out immediately and drain on a tray.
15. Untie the topigs, invert them onto a platter, and let cool.
16. Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with cinnamon or paprika, and garnish with lemon wedges and parsley. Slice into thick pieces to serve.
NOTE: Ready-skinned chick peas are available at some Armenian and Greek food stores.
